title: Email Marketing Prompt Studio
kicker: Guided prompt system for email that sells
subtitle: Built for owners and marketers who need emails with a clear job. Plan the campaign, write the message, diagnose the numbers, and save the prompts your team will use again.

profile.label: What you sell and who buys it
profile.placeholder: Example: a $3,000 operations advisory package for owner-led manufacturers with 20 to 100 employees
profile.helper: One or two sentences. Every prompt in this studio uses this.

profile.label: Your buyer in their own words
profile.placeholder: Example: 'We keep fixing the same fire every Friday' is how clients describe the problem
profile.helper: Use phrases real buyers or clients say.

profile.label: The offer or project you're working on now
profile.placeholder: Example: rebuilding the spring group program before enrollment opens

profile.label: Your main goal for the next 90 days
profile.placeholder: Example: enroll 12 right-fit clients without adding more sales calls

profile.label: Voice, proof, or constraints the AI should respect
profile.placeholder: Example: plainspoken, no income claims, two-person delivery team

categories.title: Campaign Direction
categories.note: Decide who the email is for and what it must make happen.

categories.title: Emails and Sequences
categories.note: Write messages that carry one idea to one next action.

categories.title: Lifecycle Email
categories.note: Send the right message for where the subscriber stands now.

categories.title: List and Segments
categories.note: Use behavior and intent to stop sending the same message to everyone.

categories.title: Testing and Performance
categories.note: Read the numbers and test the part most likely to change the result.

categories.title: Review and Improve
categories.note: Fix the draft while keeping the offer and voice intact.

workflows.title: Campaign Direction Memo
workflows.description: Set the audience segment, offer angle, conversion action, timing, and measurement plan.
workflows.role: email strategist accountable for message fit, conversion action, and list trust, with direct responsibility for the campaign direction memo this prompt produces

fields.label: Describe the campaign, offer, audience, and timing
fields.placeholder: Example: promote a May workshop to past buyers and engaged subscribers over 10 days
fields.helper: Give the facts the output should use. Specifics beat background.

fields.label: Numbers, evidence, or limits that should shape the answer
fields.placeholder: Example: two weeks, $4,000 budget, three client quotes, legal review required
fields.helper: Skip this if the field above already covers it.

task: Read my context and isolate the facts, constraints, and unknowns that matter to this campaign direction memo.
Set the audience segment, offer angle, conversion action, timing, and measurement plan.
Make the choices explicit. Rank options where a decision is needed, and tie every recommendation to something in my context.
Flag missing evidence and assumptions separately so I can review them before using the result.
rules: Don't pad the deliverable with a general introduction or a summary that repeats it.
When two reasonable choices compete, state the tradeoff and make one recommendation.
workflows.output: a finished campaign direction memo with the decision or artifact first, supporting reasoning, next actions, and a short assumptions check

Fix a recovery email that leans on reminders while ignoring the reason checkout stopped.
Make the choices explicit. Rank options where a decision is needed, and tie every recommendation to something in my context.
Flag missing evidence and assumptions separately so I can review them before using the result.
rules: Don't pad the deliverable with a general introduction or a summary that repeats it.
When two reasonable choices compete, state the tradeoff and make one recommendation.
workflows.output: a finished cart email review with the decision or artifact first, supporting reasoning, next actions, and a short assumptions check

workflows.title: Campaign Brief Review
workflows.description: Find missing audience, offer, proof, timing, ownership, and measurement decisions.
workflows.role: senior email editor who preserves the useful idea while removing drag, confusion, and unsupported claims, with direct responsibility for the campaign brief review this prompt produces

fields.label: Paste the email material you want reviewed
fields.placeholder: Paste one email, a sequence, subject lines, or a campaign brief
fields.helper: The prompt keeps your core intent and works directly from this material.

fields.label: Numbers, evidence, or limits that should shape the answer
fields.placeholder: Example: two weeks, $4,000 budget, three client quotes, legal review required
fields.helper: Skip this if the field above already covers it.

task: Read my context and isolate the facts, constraints, and unknowns that matter to this campaign brief review.
Find missing audience, offer, proof, timing, ownership, and measurement decisions.
Make the choices explicit. Rank options where a decision is needed, and tie every recommendation to something in my context.
Flag missing evidence and assumptions separately so I can review them before using the result.
rules: Don't pad the deliverable with a general introduction or a summary that repeats it.
When two reasonable choices compete, state the tradeoff and make one recommendation.
workflows.output: a finished campaign brief review with the decision or artifact first, supporting reasoning, next actions, and a short assumptions check

advisors.title: The Campaign Editor
advisors.description: Finds the one argument the campaign should own.
advisors.role: an email campaign editor who connects audience intent, offer, proof, and timing
advisors.focus: planning an email campaign before copy starts
questions: what the reader already knows and believes
advisors.deliverable: a campaign direction, message sequence, proof plan, and risks

advisors.title: The Inbox Writer
advisors.description: Works through one email until every line earns the next.
advisors.role: a direct response email writer who values clarity, spoken rhythm, and a clean next action
questions: the one idea the reader should leave with
the action the email must earn
advisors.deliverable: a finished email plus the choices behind the subject, opening, argument, and CTA

advisors.title: The Lifecycle Planner
advisors.description: Matches messages to the subscriber's actual stage.
advisors.role: a lifecycle marketer who maps activation, adoption, retention, and winback
questions: what useful behavior should happen next
advisors.deliverable: a lifecycle plan with triggers, timing, message jobs, exits, and measures

advisors.title: The List Steward
advisors.description: Protects attention, consent, and sender reputation.
advisors.role: an email operations lead responsible for consent, segmentation, suppression, and reputation
advisors.focus: improving list quality and sending policy
questions: what engagement and purchase data exists
advisors.deliverable: a list policy, segment map, suppression rules, and the first cleanup actions

advisors.title: The Performance Doctor
advisors.description: Finds whether the problem sits in delivery, message, offer, or page.
advisors.role: an email performance analyst who diagnoses the whole path from delivery to purchase
questions: the last three comparable campaign results
what changed in audience, offer, creative, or timing
advisors.deliverable: a ranked diagnosis, evidence needed, next tests, and what to leave alone

advisors.title: The Sequence Architect
advisors.description: Makes each email add a new piece of the decision.
advisors.role: an email sequence strategist who prevents repetition and builds argument in the right order
questions: the decision deadline or natural timing
advisors.deliverable: a send-by-send sequence map with message job, proof, CTA, and timing
